subject: Mathematics
title: Fermat: The Prince of Amateurs
description: Learn Fermat: The Prince of Amateurs in one minute.

hook.text: Pierre de Fermat was a lawyer who did mathematics as a hobby—and outperformed every professional.

buildup.text: He co-founded probability theory with Pascal and pioneered number theory and analytic geometry.

discovery.text: His 'little theorem' became a pillar of cryptography 300 years later.

twist.text: He scribbled his Last Theorem in a margin, claiming a proof too large to fit. It took 358 years to solve.

climax.text: A part-time mathematician set puzzles that consumed the full-time efforts of generations.

punchline.text: History's greatest amateur kept the pros busy for centuries.
